body:
Parent epic acceptance requires all child blockers closed AND a successful live Phase A.1 matrix run. Child agent-5b6f5872 (live matrix) remains open, so acceptance is not met. Diff only records rationale; no acceptance evidence.

Cited matrix run (matrix-20260504T044909Z) has 27/27 install_fail_permanent from a missing task directory — itself a prerequisite failure. No live run has been demonstrated where ddx-agent/pi/opencode actually execute against the anchor profile, so AC is not met.

Cited matrix run (matrix-20260504T044909Z) has 27/27 install_fail_permanent from a missing task directory — itself a prerequisite failure. No live run has been demonstrated where ddx-agent/pi/opencode actually execute against the anchor profile, so AC is not met.
result_rev=1c14b06776ec4be5bc4693318b47c44b672773b3
base_rev=30e1dc6efd74815d12317c9534b015f3744d5987

Parent bead agent-d7d2e4dd still does not have acceptance evidence for a subsequent live Phase A.1 matrix run where ddx-agent, pi, and opencode execute against the anchor profile without prerequisite failures. The two direct child blocker beads are now closed: agent-73f90363 at d252fdba44fef4f6f05c1ea3f4afd84bc53674fe and agent-5b6f5872 at ee52c22f08c46a2a021568e9c86c0697444047b0. However, the only committed three-harness matrix artifact remains benchmark-results/matrix-20260504T044909Z, whose matrix.md reports 27/27 install_fail_permanent because the runner was pointed at scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/tasks/<task-id>. That is a prerequisite failure and directly matches the blocking review finding. local_checks: - test -n "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" passed in this execution environment. - command -v harbor pi opencode ddx-agent-bench passed in this execution environment. - ddx-agent-bench profiles list --work-dir . lists gpt-5-mini. - git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 checked out 53ff2b87d621bdb97b455671f2bd9728b7d86c11. - The canary task directories exist at scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/{fix-git,git-leak-recovery,log-summary-date-ranges}; the corrected matrix invocation must pass --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2, not the stale /tasks suffix. attempted_probe: A no-cost harness-label probe was started with --profiles=noop, --harnesses=ddx-agent, --reps=1, and the corrected --tasks-dir path. It entered Harbor instead of failing immediately on label resolution, proving the ddx-agent label is still accepted by the installed runner, but it did not complete within the execution window and was stopped. The incomplete probe artifacts were removed. follow_up_needed: Run the paid Phase A.1 matrix with the corrected tasks dir, publish fresh matrix.json, matrix.md, costs.json, and update docs/research/matrix-baseline-phase-a1-2026-05-04.md or a new dated memo to cite that run. The expected command shape is: HARBOR_AGENT_ARTIFACT="$PWD/fiz-linux-amd64" ddx-agent-bench matrix --subset=scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-subset-canary.json --profiles=gpt-5-mini --harnesses=ddx-agent,pi,opencode --reps=3 --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 --per-run-budget-usd=1.00 --budget-usd=32.40 --out=benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>; then ddx-agent-bench matrix-aggregate benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>.

no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Parent bead agent-d7d2e4dd still does not have acceptance evidence for a subsequent live Phase A.1 matrix run where ddx-agent, pi, and opencode execute against the anchor profile without prerequisite failures. The two direct child blocker beads are now closed: agent-73f90363 at d252fdba44fef4f6f05c1ea3f4afd84bc53674fe and agent-5b6f5872 at ee52c22f08c46a2a021568e9c86c0697444047b0. However, the only committed three-harness matrix artifact remains benchmark-results/matrix-20260504T044909Z, whose matrix.md reports 27/27 install_fail_permanent because the runner was pointed at scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/tasks/<task-id>. That is a prerequisite failure and directly matches the blocking review finding.

local_checks:
- test -n "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" passed in this execution environment.
- command -v harbor pi opencode ddx-agent-bench passed in this execution environment.
- ddx-agent-bench profiles list --work-dir . lists gpt-5-mini.
- git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 checked out 53ff2b87d621bdb97b455671f2bd9728b7d86c11.
- The canary task directories exist at scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/{fix-git,git-leak-recovery,log-summary-date-ranges}; the corrected matrix invocation must pass --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2, not the stale /tasks suffix.

attempted_probe: A no-cost harness-label probe was started with --profiles=noop, --harnesses=ddx-agent, --reps=1, and the corrected --tasks-dir path. It entered Harbor instead of failing immediately on label resolution, proving the ddx-agent label is still accepted by the installed runner, but it did not complete within the execution window and was stopped. The incomplete probe artifacts were removed.

follow_up_needed: Run the paid Phase A.1 matrix with the corrected tasks dir, publish fresh matrix.json, matrix.md, costs.json, and update docs/research/matrix-baseline-phase-a1-2026-05-04.md or a new dated memo to cite that run. The expected command shape is: HARBOR_AGENT_ARTIFACT="$PWD/fiz-linux-amd64" ddx-agent-bench matrix --subset=scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-subset-canary.json --profiles=gpt-5-mini --harnesses=ddx-agent,pi,opencode --reps=3 --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 --per-run-budget-usd=1.00 --budget-usd=32.40 --out=benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>; then ddx-agent-bench matrix-aggregate benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>.
